meaning: 阿拉曼[埃及北部一村庄](第二次世界大战战场)
examples: My father was six and a half years in the Middle East during WW2 and he was at Alamein.
In the company of an Egyptian military driver-translator-minder-handler, I traveled all over the place: Cairo, Alexandria, El Alamein, Suez and more.
At the beginning of that decade, during World War II, he was taken prisoner during the battle of El Alamein, and held for four years by the British.
